personal.name: Aniket Poojari
personal.title: AI/ML Engineer
personal.subtitle: Agentic AI Systems | LLM Fine-Tuning & Evaluation | Scalable MLOps Pipelines
bio: AI/ML Engineer with 3+ years of experience building production agentic AI systems, LLM fine-tuning pipelines, and scalable MLOps infrastructure.

Delivered measurable impact: 66% faster internal support resolution via on-premise RAG chatbot, 3x distributed training speedup on SageMaker with DeepSpeed ZeRO-2, 25% LLM cost reduction through token analytics and model caching, and 94.2% code generation Pass@1 via dual LoRA fine-tuning on Phi-3.
Specialized in MCP, multi-layer Guardrails, Red-Teaming (216 adversarial attack vectors), Fine-Tuning (LoRA/QLoRA/PEFT), and distributed training with DeepSpeed.
Focused on shipping production-ready AI that is fast, reliable, and rigorously evaluated — every deployment is gated on automated quality checks.

projects.title: Enterprise AI Assistant with MCP
projects.description: An agentic NL-to-SQL assistant using Model Context Protocol (MCP) with 3 registered tools (query, chart, report), achieving 100% agent routing accuracy and 80% SQL accuracy. Features multi-layer Input/Output Guardrails blocking 99.5% of adversarial inputs (prompt injection, PII filtering, SQL injection prevention via sandboxed dry-runs, output data masking via LLM-as-a-judge). Includes per-request token analytics and model caching that identified 25% LLM cost reduction.
keyMetrics: 25% LLM Cost Reduction via Token Analytics

projects.title: LLM Evaluation & Red-Teaming Platform
projects.description: Full-stack platform to red-team LLMs with 216 adversarial attacks across 6 categories (Injection, PII, Jailbreak, Bias, Hallucination, Harmful Content). Implements statistical A/B testing using paired t-tests and bootstrap CI, providing 95% confidence intervals and effect sizes for evidence-based model comparisons — not just leaderboard rankings. Reduced evaluation latency by 60% through parallel batch processing and SSE-based real-time progress streaming to the React frontend.

projects.title: Agentic AI Research Assistant
projects.description: An autonomous research agent with a self-reflection loop built using LangGraph, FastAPI, and Groq. Operates through a Generate-Critique-Refine workflow — producing responses from Tavily/DuckDuckGo web search, cross-referencing every claim against retrieved evidence, and re-searching when confidence falls below 0.7. Achieves 1.0 Faithfulness score on Ragas benchmark (50+ queries). Reduces research synthesis from 3+ hours to under 10 seconds. Full observability via LangSmith tracing every node transition, tool call, and self-reflection cycle.

projects.title: BiLoRA: Dual Adapter Fine-Tuning
projects.description: End-to-end MLOps pipeline fine-tuning Microsoft Phi-3-Mini (3.8B) with two specialized LoRA adapters targeting separate model components — attention layers for code generation (MBPP), MLP layers for docstring generation (CodeXGLUE). Uses 4-bit NF4 QLoRA quantization via BitsAndBytes (75% VRAM reduction), a 6-stage DVC pipeline with LLM-as-judge quality gate blocking deployment on metric regression, CI/CD auto-deploy to HF Spaces, and dynamic adapter hot-swapping in the Streamlit UI to avoid maintaining multiple model instances in memory.

projects.title: Multi-Modal RAG QA System
projects.description: Production-style RAG application that ingests PDFs (text via PyMuPDF + tables via Tabula) and websites (BeautifulSoup4), chunks and embeds content with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 into ChromaDB, and answers queries via Groq LLaMA 3.1. Detects cross-document comparative questions and balances retrieval across sources. Each response shows inline latency, chunks retrieved, and token usage. Thumbs-up/down feedback stored in HF Dataset, with a Streamlit monitoring dashboard visualizing latency trends, token costs, and retrieval quality.

projects.title: Text-to-Image Generation with Diffusion Models
projects.description: End-to-end latent diffusion pipeline — from data preparation and Florence-2 automated captioning through distributed training on AWS SageMaker (DeepSpeed ZeRO Stage 2), experiment tracking with MLflow, and inference optimization (ONNX / TensorRT, 4x throughput). Custom UNet with cross-attention, Stable Diffusion VAE for 8x latent compression, and CLIP text conditioning. Critical training bottleneck eliminated by precomputing CLIP embeddings and caching to S3 — achieving 100x data loading speedup enabling stable multi-node training.

projects.title: Monocular Depth Estimation
projects.description: DPT model (ViT-Base/16 encoder + convolutional fusion decoder) predicting metric depth from single RGB images on Cityscapes (max depth 80m). Trained with scale-invariant + gradient loss, differential learning rates (1e-5 ViT / 1e-4 decoder), AMP, cosine LR warmup, and early stopping. 6-stage DVC pipeline: preprocessing → training → evaluate → conditional model promotion (MLflow) → ONNX export → push to HF Hub. Gradio demo auto-deployed to HF Spaces via GitHub Actions using ONNX Runtime for CPU inference.

projects.title: Automated Stock Price Prediction
projects.description: CNN-based stock price prediction system with dynamic indicator selection via XGBoost feature engineering. Fetches historical data from Yahoo Finance API, performs comprehensive EDA, and trains models tracked via MLflow with DVC pipeline orchestration.

projects.title: Lane Segmentation
projects.description: Semantic segmentation for lane detection in road images using pre-trained architectures from segmentation-models-pytorch. Custom LaneDataset class with horizontal flip augmentation, trained on 224x224 images normalized with ImageNet statistics. Achieves 80% IoU for autonomous driving lane detection.

projects.title: Motor Self Inspection
projects.description: A web app that detects vehicle damages for faster insurance claim approvals using Mask R-CNN for damage detection.

projects.title: Image Denoising
projects.description: Variational autoencoder for image denoising, reconstructing clean images from noisy inputs.

projects.title: NYC Taxi Trip Duration Prediction
projects.description: Prediction system estimating taxi trip durations in NYC using CatBoost with XGBoost-assisted feature selection.

projects.title: Texture Generation (DC-GAN)
projects.description: Designed and implemented a DC-GAN to generate realistic textures from scratch.

projects.title: Russian-to-English Translation
projects.description: Encoder-decoder model with attention mechanism for Russian-to-English text translation.

projects.title: Human-Computer Interaction (Gestures + Voice)
projects.description: Interactive system enabling computer control using hand gestures and voice commands. Published in IEEE Xplore.

projects.title: Cricket Shot Classification
projects.description: Keypoint R-CNN (pretrained on COCO) extracts body keypoints, which train a neural network for classifying cricket shot poses.

projects.title: Text Summarization GPT from Scratch
projects.description: Custom 125M-parameter GPT model pretrained and fine-tuned on the XSum dataset using teacher forcing. Two-phase training pipeline with 128-token context window, orchestrated via DVC with MLflow experiment tracking. Achieved ROUGE-L score of 0.54 on validation.

highlights: Building demand forecasting models for 8,000+ SKUs across 3 regional distribution centers using XGBoost and ARIMA time-series analysis, targeting 35% reduction in weekly planning effort through automated restock recommendations.

Replaced 80% of manual spreadsheet-based procurement workflows by engineering automated data pipelines ingesting 200K+ daily transactions from 12 supplier sources into a centralized SQL feature store enabling ML-driven restocking decisions.
Building a real-time supply chain dashboard with route clustering, targeting 20% improvement in logistics efficiency and reduction in regional shipping delays across distribution centers.

Reduced internal team support resolution time by 66% (3 days → 1 day) by building a secure on-premise RAG chatbot for Kritova's internal use — an EdTech startup building online learning platforms — maintaining 100% data privacy compliance with zero data leaving the firewall.

Built a 4-microservice ML platform serving 300+ concurrent students on Kritova's e-learning platform, with automated data pipelines for course analytics and real-time performance tracking across 50+ learning modules.

Designed a multi-modal document ingestion pipeline (PyMuPDF for text extraction, Tabula for PDF table parsing) enabling cross-document comparative queries across course materials — previously impossible with keyword search alone.
Identified 25% LLM cost reduction by engineering per-request token analytics and a model caching layer tracking spend per query type across sessions.

Achieved 88% accuracy in movement intention prediction from EEG brain signals for mobility-impaired patients by building a Python deep learning pipeline (CNNs) across 3 public neurological datasets — enabling potential touchless device control for people with physical disabilities.
Reduced data preprocessing time by 50% by developing reusable standardization tools for OpenBCI headset data, enabling 3x faster experimentation across 12 signal processing configurations.

Standardized 15+ PyTorch and Scikit-learn lab notebooks into reproducible ML pipelines for 300+ students across 5 courses, reducing setup failures — spanning GPU driver conflicts, Python version mismatches, dependency errors, and GCP/Colab access issues — by 35%.
Maintained 99% uptime for GPU-based ML training environments on GCP and Google Colab, supporting 10,000+ GPU compute hours across Deep Learning and Cloud Computing courses.
Improved block-trade allocation accuracy by 20% and reduced error rates by 15% using Random Forest and XGBoost models on high-frequency trading data.
Built Python/Bash automation for trade metrics (Sharpe ratio, PnL parsing), accelerating model evaluation cycles by 15x — reducing manual analysis from 1-2 hours to under 10 minutes per strategy.
Processed 50GB+ of daily trading logs using Apache Spark, detecting critical data discrepancies and boosting strategy testing accuracy by 50%.
Built automated trade-cleansing pipelines flagging anomalous entries in real-time, reducing manual review workload by 30% and improving downstream model reliability by 25%.
Automated vehicle damage detection in insurance claims by training a Mask R-CNN model, raising detection accuracy by 18% and cutting manual claim processing by 45%.
Integrated ML workflows into the Django-based claims system, reducing overall claim processing time by 35%.

testimonials.name: Vivek Kashyap
testimonials.content: Aniket is well focused, hardworking as well as a solution oriented guy. In his term of internship he and team has solved the assigned problem very well before the given timeline. The assigned project was designed and developed by them as expected and meeting all the requirements. I'll highly recommend him, he is good team contributor, very productive, good and can be great problem solver. Best Wishes
